messages.content: Create a 40-second whiteboard explainer video for an HR workflow automation service used by growing companies. The video should explain how the platform simplifies employee onboarding, document collection, approval routing, and compliance tracking. Use a clean whiteboard style with hand-drawn black outlines, simple blue accent highlights, arrows, icons, and step-by-step scene progression. The storytelling should move in a clear order: HR pain points, workflow bottlenecks, automated onboarding flow, compliance visibility, and time savings for HR teams. Keep it simple, educational, and easy to follow.

skills_metadata.name: creative-skill
skills_metadata.description: Develop creative direction from user intent — concept previews, creative scripts, subject element mapping, and production model selection (four-layer routing). Use when the idea is vague, direction needs confirmation, or references need creative interpretation. Not for confirmed briefs (generation-skill) or fixing existing videos (modification-skill).

skills_metadata.name: generation-skill
skills_metadata.description: Execute full video production from a confirmed creative brief. Handles sequence planning, model routing (Seedance / Kling / Grok — four-layer system), generation, and post-assembly audio (BGM + off-screen narration only). Use when creative direction is confirmed and production is needed. Not for vague ideas (creative-skill) or modifying existing videos (modification-skill).

skills_metadata.name: modification-skill
skills_metadata.description: Modify an existing produced video based on user feedback — re-generate (Seedance preferred, Kling for real-face/IP/fine-detail), visual-edit, or post-production adjust specific parts, then reassemble with audio. Use when the user wants changes to an existing video. Not for first-time creation (creative/generation-skill) or concept preview redos (creative-skill).
